revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine |image=File:Iyozuhiko no mikoto jinja haiden.jpg |location={{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama|1=Matsuyama}}, Iaisou 2-2-1 |ISO=JP-38 |latitude degrees=33|Rank minutes=48|Rank seconds=33 |longitude degrees=132|longitude minutes=46|longitude seconds=15 |deity={{ill|Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|伊豫豆比古命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505756|1=Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Iyozuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|伊豫豆比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyozuhime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505758|1=Iyozuhime-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Iyonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊与主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyonushi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505760|1=Iyonushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Ehime-no-Mikoto|ja|愛比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}
Old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}
{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|qid=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} |founded=Traditional date during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|qid=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}} |festival=Spring festival (old calendar January 8)
Autumn festival (October 6) |main hall=Ōji style |other names='''Tsubaki Shrine''' |ceremonies=Tsubaki festival (old calendar January 7-9) |map=Japan Matsuyama city |prefecture={{ill|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|1=Matsuyama}} |coordinates={{Coord|33.80916666666667|N|132.77083333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Imperial Seal of Japan.svg|thumb|130px|right|Shrine Crest (Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum)]] '''Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine'''（伊豫豆比古命神社）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Iaisou Town, {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama|1=Matsuyama}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and an old {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. The shrine crest is a sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. Locally, it is also known as '''Tsubaki Shrine''' or '''O-Tsubaki-san'''. The shrine is sometimes spelled with づ tsu and sometimes with a ず. The kana are pronounced identically in most dialects of Japanese though. The shrine officially uses づ, == deity == * {{ill|Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|伊豫豆比古命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505756|1=Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto}} (male deity) * {{ill|Iyozuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|伊豫豆比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyozuhime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505758|1=Iyozuhime-no-Mikoto}} (female deity) * {{ill|Iyonushi-no-Mikoto|ja|伊与主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyonushi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505760|1=Iyonushi-no-Mikoto}} (male deity) * {{ill|Ehime-no-Mikoto|ja|愛比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ehime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (female deity) According to the "{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}" and "{{ill|Kujiki|lt=Kujiki|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko hongi|qq=}}," Iyonushi-no-Mikoto is the first generation of the {{ill|Kume clan|lt=Kume clan|lt=Kumi no Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|久味国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumi no Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=}}. There are theories that Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto and Iyonushi-no-Mikoto are the same or that Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto is the ancestor deity and Iyonushi-no-Mikoto is his successor. Additionally, the name of Ehime Prefecture comes from Ehime-no-Mikoto, and Ehime is the only prefecture in Japan named after a deity. == History == In ancient times, the area around the shrine was a sea, and it was called "Tsu-waki Shrine" (Tsu meaning "sea"). Over time, this changed to "Tsubaki Shrine." Additionally, various types of camellia (椿) trees, including Japanese camellia, grow naturally around the shrine, which contributed to its current name. According to shrine tradition, it was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|qid=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}}. The 2250th anniversary of its founding was celebrated in 1962 (Showa 37), and the 2300th anniversary in 2012 (Heisei 24). It is identified as the Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine listed in the "{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}" (a small shrine). However, it is also speculated to be the major shrine of Iyo, but the leading contender for that designation is {{ill|Iyo Shrine|ja|伊予神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Matsumae Town|ja|松前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumae Town|1=松前町|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1307271}}, Iyo District. In the Edo period, it received fervent devotion from the {{ill|Hisamatsu Clan|ja|久松氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisamatsu Clan|1=Hisamatsu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369441}}, the lords of the {{ill|Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|en|Iyo-Matsuyama Domain|qid=Q1042087|lt=Matsuyama Domain|1=Iyo-Matsuyama Domain}}. Today, the shrine is widely respected for bringing good luck and prosperity, with worshippers from all over the country. == Outer and Inner Grounds == * Shrine name Board: A large stone monument at the Tsubaki Shrine Entrance Intersection, where National Route 33 and Prefectural Route 19 intersect. * East Grand Torii Gate: A giant red torii gate spanning Prefectural Route 19 on the east side. * West Grand Torii Gate: A giant red torii gate spanning Prefectural Route 19 on the west side. * Grand Torii: The torii gate at the entrance to the shrine grounds. * Main Gate (Zuishinmon): Statues of the right and left ministers. * Main Shrine: Haiden, Honden, and corridors. * Tsubaki Prayer Hall: Built in Heisei 12 (2000) on the site of the former Dance Hall (1837-2000). It serves as the shrine office and waiting room. * Sub-Shrine, Soja Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Shionaru Takutsuna no Okina no Kami|ja|潮鳴栲綱翁神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shionaru Takutsuna no Okina no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to legend, when Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto and Iyozuhime-no-Mikoto docked their ship at Mount Funayama, this deity welcomed them. Therefore, it is customary to first visit Soja Shrine when worshipping at Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine. * Sub-Shrine, Katsuyuki Hachiman Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) and {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}. It was established to commemorate the military victories of the {{ill|Kono clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kono clan|1=Kōno clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11554232}} during the {{ill|Mongol invasions of Japan|en|Mongol invasions of Japan|qid=Q208156|lt=Mongol Invasions of Japan|1=Mongol invasions of Japan}}. Known as "Shogun-san," it is revered for success in battles, exams, and academics. * Sub-Shrine, Mikura Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It is the local deity of the Iaisou district, protecting the prosperity of businesses and family fortunes. * Sub-Shrine, Komori Shrine: Dedicated to {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Heavenly Water Distribution deity) and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. These deities are revered for child-rearing and safe childbirth. After a child's [[Miyamairi|first shrine visit]], parents pray for their child's growth and offer {{ill|bib|en|bib|qid=Q407378|lt=bibs|1=Bib}}. File:Iyozu_01.jpg|Grand Torii and Main Gate File:Iyozu 03.jpg|Katsuyuki Hachiman Shrine File:Iyozu 02.jpg|Mikura Shrine File:Iyozu_04.jpg|Komori Shrine File:Iyozu-hiko-no-Mikoto-jinja 20240625 (3).jpg|Shrine Grounds == Cultural Properties == ; Matsuyama City Designated Tangible Cultural Property * Guardian Dogs: A pair of lion-shaped statues, 64cm and 67cm tall, from the late Muromachi period. Displayed to the right of Komori Shrine. Designated on March 25, 1977 (Showa 52). == annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! ceremony |- | January 1 || {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, Young Water Drawing ceremony |- | January || Sub-Shrine, Katsuyuki Hachiman Shrine festival |- | Lunisolar January 7-9 || Tsubaki festival (Spring Grand festival) |- | February 3 || {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分}} |- | February 11 || {{ill|Kigensetsu|ja|紀元祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀元節}} |- | Vernal Equinox Day || Spring {{ill|Kōreisai|en|Kōreisai|qid=Q6455253|lt=Kōreisai|1=Kōreisai}} Distant Worship ceremony |- | Late March || Worshippers' Grand festival |- | April 3 || {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Festival|ja|神武天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu-tennō-sai|qq=}} Distant Worship ceremony |- | April 29 || {{ill|Shōwa Emperor|ja|昭和祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa Emperor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- | Late April || World Peace Harmony Stone festival |- | May 1 || Satsuki {{ill|Koromogae|ja|衣替え|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koromogae|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Seasonal wardrobe change in Japan|qid=Q3198999}} Shrine Garment festival |- | May 5 || {{ill|Dragon Boat Festival|en|Dragon Boat Festival|qid=Q1254268|lt=Dragon Boat Festival|1=Dragon Boat Festival}} Seasonal festival |- | Early June || Ishii District War Dead Memorial festival |- | Early June || Rice Planting festival (held in {{ill|Ōzu, Ehime|en|Ōzu, Ehime|qid=Q386655|lt=Ōzu|1=Ōzu, Ehime}}) |- | July 31 || Nagoshi festival (Fumizuki {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|en|Ōharae-shiki|qid=Q11261910|lt=Ōharae|1=Ōharae-shiki}} ceremony) |- | September || Brush Mound Brush Spirit festival |- | Mid-September || Respect for the Aged festival |- | Autumnal Equinox Day || Autumn Kōreisai Distant Worship ceremony |- | Late September || Rice Harvest festival (held in Ōzu) |- | October 1 || Kannazuki Shrine Garment festival |- | October 6 || Autumn {{ill|festival|ja|festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フェスティバル|1=Festival (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q304952}} Grand festival |- | October 7 || {{ill|Shinko-shiki|en|Shinko-shiki|qid=Q123907414|lt=Shinko-shiki|1=Shinko-shiki}}, Otabisho festival, Return festival |- | October 17 || {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|qid=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai Festival|1=Kannamesai Festival}} Distant Worship ceremony |- | November 3 || {{ill|Meiji Emperor's Birthday|ja|明治祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Emperor's Birthday|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- | November 23 || {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} |- | December 23 || {{ill|Emperor's Birthday|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor's Birthday|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q72318}} |- | December 31 || Shiwasu Great Purification ceremony, {{ill|New Year's Eve|ja|除夜祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Eve|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- | 1st and 15th of every month || Monthly festivals |} == Tsubaki festival == Held on the old calendar January 7-9. In the past, the festival was held only on the old calendar January 8 and was called "O-Yōka" (おようか). Today, it is held over three days (January 7-9). It is also known as "O-Tsubaki-san" and "The festival that Calls Spring to Iyo." During the festival, tens of thousands of people visit, making it the largest festival in Shikoku in terms of the number of visitors. The ceremonies during the festival include the opening of the gates at midnight on the 7th, the Spring Grand festival at 9 AM on the 8th, the rear shrine festival at 6 PM on the 9th, and the closing of the gates at midnight. Additionally, special ceremonies such as the Lending Money ceremony, the Secret Procession, and the Combined Fire are held. The Lending Money ceremony involves borrowing a sacred coin (20 yen) from the shrine and returning double the amount the following year, symbolizing a promise to work diligently and repay more. The Secret Procession occurs on the night of the 8th, where the shrine's portable shrine is carried to the {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in Kitadoi Town. The procession is carried out quietly until the portable shrine passes through the main gate, hence the name "Secret Procession." The Combined Fire, also known as the Five Feast Fires, involves residents along the procession route burning their New Year's decorations to welcome the portable shrine. == Access == * {{ill|Iyotetsu Bus|ja|伊予鉄バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyotetsu Bus|1=Iyotetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q53308357}} ** Tsubaki-mae Bus Stop (15-minute intervals during the day). ** Furukawa Yokota Bus Stop (Ichitsubo/Hanamizuki Line). * {{ill|JR Shikoku Bus|ja|JR四国バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Shikoku Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ジェイアール四国バス|1=JR Shikoku Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10851154}} ** Kumakogen Line Tsubaki-no-Miya Bus Stop. == Notes == * In 2013, to commemorate the 2300th anniversary of the shrine's establishment, paintings and sliding door paintings by artist {{ill|Taisuke Hamada|ja|浜田泰介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisuke Hamada|1=Taisuke Hamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557983}} and sculptures by sculptor {{ill|Nobuyuki Ishimizu|ja|石水信至|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobuyuki Ishimizu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were dedicated to the shrine. * The honorary chief priest, Nobuaki Chōsokabe, served as the chairman of the {{ill|Shinto Seiji Renmei|ja|神道政治連盟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Seiji Renmei|1=Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590929}} and as a representative of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|en|Nippon Kaigi|qid=Q11507216|lt=Nippon Kaigi|1=Nippon Kaigi}} until 2016. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Hio Hachiman Shrine|ja|日尾八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hio Hachiman Shrine|1=Higashidōgo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11505266}} (Tōdō Shrine) * {{ill|Goshiki Sōmen|ja|五色素麺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshiki Sōmen|qq=}} - A specialty of Matsuyama said to have been devised in connection with this shrine. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Iyozu-hiko-no-Mikoto-jinja}} * [http://tubaki.or.jp/ Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine - Tsubaki Shrine] * [https://www.iyokannet.jp/spot/1802 Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine (Tsubaki Shrine) | Iyo Tourism Net] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|伊豫豆比古命神社|version=104514108|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Iyozu-hiko-no-Mikoto-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine (Q11380620)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iyo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|lt=Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|伊豫豆比古命神社|commons|Category:Iyozu-hiko-no-Mikoto-jinja|qid=Q11380620|1=Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || — || — || {{ill|Iyotsuhikono- Shrine|lt=Iyotsuhikono- Shrine|qid=Q135041437|1=Iyotsuhikono- Shrine}} || {{coord|33.809315|132.770971|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183268 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11380620}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q11380620]] [[ja:伊豫豆比古命神社]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
